revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]][[陰陽寮]]へのリダイレクト
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[陰陽寮]]

'''陰陽博士'''（おんみょうはかせ）は、[[律令制]]で[[陰陽寮]]に設置された教官の1つ。[[陰陽生]]を教育することを掌った。定員1名（ただし実数には異説あり）。[[正七位下]]相当。
『[[日本書紀]]』[[持統天皇]]6年（[[692年]]）の条が初出。ただし、定員については『[[続日本紀]]』[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）に[[大津首]]・[[津守通]]の両名が陰陽博士として登場しており、実際には定員が複数だったとする見方もある。陰陽生10名を教育したが、[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）に陰陽寮強化の一環としてうち3名を[[陰陽得業生]]として給費し、更に後には博士に対して[[職田]]4町が与えられた。教科書としては、子部五行類の『[[新撰陰陽書]]』・『[[黄帝金匱経]]』・『[[五行大義]]』・『[[周易]]』・『[[難儀]]』などが用いられた。[[奈良時代]]から[[平安時代]]初期にかけては、[[大津氏]]・[[弓削氏]]・[[滋岳氏]]などが、平安中期には[[惟宗氏]]・[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]・[[賀茂氏]]などが[[世業]]としていたが、平安後期以後は安倍氏と賀茂氏による[[世襲]]となった。

revision.comment: +出典の明記

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
==In media== === Books === * ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Aramata]], published in 1985 * ''[[Onmyōji (novel series)|Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Baku Yumemakura]], published in 1988 * ''[[The Summer of the Ubume]]'', ''[[Mōryō no Hako]]'' and other Kyōgokudō series, written by [[Natsuhiko Kyogoku]], published in 1994 * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Miyako|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'', written by [[Soichiro Watase]], published in 2001 * ''[[Shōnen Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Mitsuru Yūki]], published in 2001 * ''[[Rental Magica]]'', written by {{ill|Makoto Sanda|ja|三田誠}}, published in 2004 * ''{{ill|Bakemonogatari|ja|化物語}}'', written by [[Nisio Isin]], published in 2005 * ''[[Fairy Navigator Runa]]'', written by {{ill|Miyoko Ikeda|ja|池田美代子}}, published in 2008 * ''[[Tokyo Ravens]]'', written by {{ill|Kōhei Azano|ja|あざの耕平}}, published in 2010
=== Manga and anime ===
* ''[[Tokyo Babylon]]'', written by [[Clamp (manga artists)|Clamp]], published in 1990 * ''[[X (manga)|X]]'', written by Clamp, published in 1992 * ''[[Shaman King]]'', written by [[Hiroyuki Takei]], published in 1998 * ''[[Gag Manga Biyori]]'', written by [[Kōsuke Masuda]], published in 2000 * ''[[Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi]]'', created by [[Gainax]], published in 2001 * ''[[Amatsuki]]'', written by {{ill|Shinobu Takayama|ja|高山しのぶ}}, published in 2002 * ''[[Gin Tama]]'', written by [[Hideaki Sorachi]], published in 2003 * ''[[xxxHolic]]'', written by Clamp, published in 2003 * ''[[Onmyō Taisenki]]'', written by Yoshihiko Tomizawa, published in 2003 * ''[[Musashi Gundoh]]'', based on an unused story by [[Monkey Punch]], published in 2006 * ''{{ill|Shibariya Komachi|ja|縛り屋小町}}'', written by [[Mick Takeuchi]], published in 2006 * ''[[Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Shiibashi]], published in 2008 * ''[[Twin Star Exorcists]]'', written by {{ill|Yoshiaki Sukeno|ja|助野嘉昭}}, published in 2013 * ''[[The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World |The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World]]'', written by Kiichi Kosuzu, published in 2018 * ''[[MAO (manga)|MAO]]'', written by [[Rumiko Takahashi]], published in 2019
=== Films === *''[[Tokyo: The Last Megalopolis]]'', a 1988 Japanese fantasy film directed by [[Akio Jissōji]], based on the novel ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'' by Hiroshi Aramata *[[Onmyōji (film)|''Onmyōji'' (film)]], a 2001 Japanese film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura and released in the U.S. in 2003 as ''Onmyōji - The Yin Yang Master'' **''[[Onmyōji 2]]'', the 2003 sequel *''[[The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity]]'', a 2020 Chinese fantasy film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura directed by [[Guo Jingming]], and starring [[Mark Chao]] and [[Deng Lun]] *''[[The Yinyang Master]]'', a 2021 Chinese fantasy film directed by Li Weiran, and starring [[Chen Kun]], [[Zhou Xun]] and [[William Chan]] based on the game that was based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura
=== Video games === * ''{{ill|Kuon no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'', developed by [[FOG Inc.]], released in 1998 * ''[[Tokyo Majin Gakuen]]'', produced by {{ill|Shūhō Imai|ja|今井秋芳}}, released in 1998 * ''[[Kuon]]'', developed by [[FromSoftware]], released in 2004 * ''[[11eyes: Tsumi to Batsu to Aganai no Shōjo]]'', developed by {{ill|Lass (video game developer)|lt=Lass|ja|Lass}}, released in 2008 * ''[[Naraka: Bladepoint]]'', developed by [[24 Entertainment]], released in 2021 features the character [[Kurumi Tsuchimikado]], who according to game lore is the first female Onmyōji, descended from a long line of Onmyōdō masters.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.narakathegame.com/index.html|title = Naraka: Bladepoint - New Hero Out Now!}}</ref>
== See also ==
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[Category:dd]][[ja:陰陽博士]]
== See also ==

|publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=4827310572}} * {{cite book |last=Endō |first=Katsumi |year=1994 |title=近世陰陽道史の研究 |trans-title=Studies in the History of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher={{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōrai Sha|ja|新人物往来社}} |isbn=4404021569}} * {{cite book |last=Kosaka |first=Shinji |year=2004 |title=安倍晴明撰『占事略決』と陰陽道 |trans-title=Abe |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Sagano Shoin |isbn=4782303610}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2005 |title=平安貴族と陰陽師 |trans-title=Heian Nobles and Onmyōji |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b34635.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642079424}} * {{cite book |last=Hayashi |first=Jun |year=2005 |title=近世陰陽道の研究 |trans-title=A Study of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b32982.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642034072}} * {{cite book revision.comment: some pages here lack the tag but to my knowledge everything here eitherr is an original page or at least worth preserving while I potentially trirm the wikipedia derived articles
revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[Category:dd]][[ja:陰陽博士]]

|publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=4827310572}} * {{cite book |last=Endō |first=Katsumi |year=1994 |title=近世陰陽道史の研究 |trans-title=Studies in the History of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[ja:陰陽博士]]

{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```

From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Original pages]] to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted link categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[ja:陰陽博士]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted link categories]] to [[Category:bump 1-20]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[ja:陰陽博士]]

|last=Yamashita |first=Katsuaki |year=1996 |title=平安時代の宗教文化と陰陽道 |trans-title=Religious Culture in the Heian Period and Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Iwata Shoin |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Sagano Shoin |isbn=4782303610}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2005 |title=平安貴族と陰陽師 |trans-title=Heian Nobles and Onmyōji |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b34635.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642079424}} * {{cite book |last=Hayashi |first=Jun |year=2005 |title=近世陰陽道の研究 |trans-title=A Study of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b32982.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642034072}} * {{cite book |publisher=[[Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-Shinsha]] |isbn=4121018443}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 1 古代 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 1: The Ancient Times |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017970}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 2 中世 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 2: The Middle Ages |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017987}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 3 近世 revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[ja:陰陽博士]]

In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[ja:陰陽博士]]

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```

{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.

From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
[[ja:陰陽博士]]

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onmyō Hakase}} [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Onmyō Hakase-qq]] [[Category:Onmyō Hakase]][[Category:Defunct government positions in Japan]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} ```
