{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|ja|陰陽博士}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|陰陽博士" to "|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase"
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天文博士" to "|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase"
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|漏刻博士" to "|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase"
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]][[en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]] [[ja:陰陽博士]] Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year }} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
<!--Onmyō Hakase-->

{{cite book |editor-last=[[Seimei Shrine]] |first= |year=2002 |title=安倍晴明公 |trans-title=The Right Honorable Abe no Seimei |url=https://bookclub.kodansha.co.jp/product?item=0000180822 |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Sagano Shoin |isbn=4782303610}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2005 |title=平安貴族と陰陽師 |trans-title=Heian Nobles and Onmyōji |publisher=[[Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-Shinsha]] |isbn=4121018443}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 1 古代 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 1: The Ancient The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=draft}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=draft}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=draft}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=draft}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=draft}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=draft}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=draft}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=draft}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=draft}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Onmyō Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Onmyō Hakase|Onmyō Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=draft}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=draft}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=draft}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=draft}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=draft}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=draft}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=draft}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=draft}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=draft}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=draft}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=draft}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=draft}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=draft}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=draft}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=draft}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=draft}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=draft}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=draft}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=draft}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=draft}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=draft}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=draft}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=draft}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=draft}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=draft}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=draft}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=draft}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite needed|date=January 2023}} '''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=draft}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}}. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}.
The role first appeared in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=draft}}, though the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=draft}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=draft}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders.
The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=draft}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|730}}), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=draft}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=draft}} (allotted rice fields).
Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=draft}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}, {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=draft}}.
During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=draft}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.
