{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|qq=|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|qq=|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|qq=|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|qq=|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|qq=|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|qq=|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|qq=|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|qq=|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11658257]]
Wikidata name: onmyō hakase
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. {{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Unreferenceded|date=January 2023}}
'''Onmyō Hakase''' ({{ill|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/陰陽博士|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}) was an instructional position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. Its primary role was to educate [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. The position was held by one appointee (though there are alternative views on the actual number). It was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.

The role first appeared in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in an entry for {{ill|6th year of Emperor Jitō|ja|692|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th year of Emperor Jitō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=692|WD=Q3265157}}, though the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records for {{ill|5th year of Yōrō|ja|721|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th year of Yōrō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 700|1=700|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q721126}} mention two individuals, {{ill|Ōtsu no Kubi|ja|大津首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu no Kubi|1=Ōtsu no Kubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51574202}} and {{ill|Tsumori Michi|ja|津守通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Michi|1=Tsumori Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51166254}}, serving as Onmyō Hakase, suggesting the position may have had multiple holders. 

The Onmyō Hakase was responsible for educating ten {{ill|Onmyōsei|ja|陰陽生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students. However, in {{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:730|730]]), as part of efforts to strengthen the Onmyōryō, three of these students were designated as {{ill|Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja|陰陽得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Tokugyōsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and received stipends. These students were subsequently chosen for future positions as Onmyōji and Onmyō Hakase. Additionally, the Hakase was granted four chō (町) of {{ill|Shikiden|ja|職田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職分田|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}} (allotted rice fields). 

Educational texts included {{ill|Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja|新撰陰陽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Onmyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja|黄帝金匱経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huangdi Jinkui Jing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gogyō Daiyi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daiyi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}, [[:en:I Ching|I Ching]], and {{ill|Nangi|ja|難儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangi|qq=}}. 

During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] and early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the position was typically held by members of the {{ill|Ōtsu clan|ja|大津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shigatake clan|ja|滋岳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigatake clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Imibe clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} became prominent. From the late Heian period onwards, the position became hereditary, monopolized by the Abe and Kamo families.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
